headline: Renewable Energy Target of 31% by 2025 Achieves Only 24%
context: Malaysia's renewable energy mix reached 24% of installed capacity by end-2025, missing the 31% target under the National Energy Policy, with solar farm approvals delayed by land acquisition disputes and grid connectivity bottlenecks.

cards.big: 7 percentage points short — Malaysia's 31% renewable energy target missed as solar approvals stuck in bureaucratic limbo
cards.sub: Only 2,800 MW of 5,200 MW approved large solar projects actually connected to grid by December 2025

cards.big: Solar energy cost in Malaysia at 18 sen/kWh is now cheaper than new gas plants at 24 sen/kWh
cards.sub: Grid infrastructure can't absorb intermittent solar — TNB's grid upgrade investment at RM2.1 billion covers only 35% of need

cards.big: Net Energy Metering scheme benefits only 72,000 rooftop solar adopters — overwhelmingly T20 homeowners
cards.sub: B40 households in flats and low-cost housing structurally excluded from solar self-generation benefits

cards.big: Energy Commission approved solar farm licences require 14 separate agency clearances averaging 22 months
cards.sub: SEDA, EC, TNB, DOE, state land office, and 9 other agencies each hold independent veto power over projects

cards.big: Solar is now cheaper than gas — the only thing blocking Malaysia's energy transition is 14 layers of bureaucracy
Should Malaysia create a one-stop renewable energy authority to consolidate all 14 approval agencies into one 6-month process?
